name: Electrical
description_short: Electrical Workers Local 58 Credit Union is a membership-based financial institution serving electrical workers and their families with savings, checking, loans, and credit card products.
description_long:
Electrical Workers Local 58 Credit Union (EWCU) is a credit union headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, at 1366 Porter Street. The institution serves members of Local 58 of the International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers and their families, operating as a member-owned cooperative financial institution. EWCU recently completed a system upgrade on November 1, 2025, requiring all members to create new online banking accounts.

EWCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking products including savings accounts, checking accounts, auto loans, recreational loans, personal loans, and a Visa credit card. Their featured rates include 4.99% APR on new car loans, 5.49% APR on used car loans, 8.99% APR on personal loans, 5.49% APR on recreational loans, and 10.90% APR on their Visa card with no annual fee. Members can access these products through online applications, a mobile app (IT'S ME 247), phone banking, and in-person at their single physical location.

As a credit union, EWCU distinguishes itself through member-owned governance, where profits theoretically benefit member-owners rather than external shareholders. The institution emphasizes personalized service with experienced staff and positions itself as offering competitive rates for auto financing. EWCU maintains limited branch presence with only one physical location and operates restricted hours (Monday-Friday 9am-4pm, closed weekends and Saturdays with a lunch closure on Fridays 11:30am-12:30pm).

EWCU is a smaller, community-focused credit union with membership restrictions tied to electrical worker affiliation. While they offer competitive auto loan rates and no-fee credit card products, the single branch location and limited operating hours may be inconvenient for members. The institution recently underwent a system migration, which temporarily disrupted member access and required account recreation. For electricians and their families seeking local banking with competitive auto financing, EWCU can be viable, but the geographic and time-based limitations and membership eligibility restrictions should be considered. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Visa credit card with no annual fee (10.90% APR)
pros: Competitive auto loan rates at 4.99% for new cars and 5.49% for used cars
Visa credit card with no annual fee at 10.90% APR
Member-owned credit union structure with emphasis on member benefits
Mobile app (IT'S ME 247) available for account access and management
Recreation loan product at 5.49% APR for recreational vehicle financing
Online account opening and loan applications available
cons: Single physical branch location in Detroit limits accessibility for members outside Michigan
Restricted operating hours (9am-4pm weekdays only, closed weekends) limit member access
Membership eligibility restricted to electrical workers and their families, not open to general public
Recent system upgrade (11/01/2025) required all members to create new accounts, causing service disruption
No information provided about savings account rates or CD products despite offering savings accounts
best_for: Electrical workers and their immediate family members seeking auto financing
Members in the Detroit, Michigan area with regular access during business hours
Credit union members prioritizing member-owned governance and competitive rates over branch convenience
Individuals needing recreational vehicle loans or personal loans with fixed rates
diagnosis: Electrical Workers Local 58 Credit Union is best for electrical workers and their families in the Detroit area seeking competitive auto loan rates and member-owned banking benefits. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to electrical workers and their families, and the single Detroit branch with limited hours (closed weekends) makes it inaccessible for members outside Michigan or those needing evening/weekend banking.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
